definition: The process or mode of becoming conditioned by clinging and leading onward to birth.
notes: The project prefers becoming because it preserves the dynamic force of the term in dependent origination. Existence can be useful in some explanatory contexts, but as a default it risks sounding too static or metaphysical. The term should be heard as an ongoing process sustained by clinging and issuing in birth, not as a static metaphysical state. Buddhadasa Bhikkhu often explains this link in strongly practical terms as the becoming that culminates in the fresh birth of I and mine; the repository records that as a source-backed explanatory emphasis.

context_rules.context: In most dependent-origination and doctrinal contexts
In explanatory prose where the mode of existence is under discussion
context_rules.notes: Acceptable when it improves readability without losing process.

context_rules.context: When the passage emphasizes the link from clinging into birth in the standard sequence
context_rules.notes: Keep the processive force of the term explicit.
Notes may explicitly connect becoming to egoic or affective birth rather than only biological rebirth.

example_phrases.notes: Shows the term tied directly to craving.
